issue_title: CVE-2023-0482- Creation of Temporary File With Insecure Permissions vulnerability in org.jboss.resteasy:resteasy-multipart-provider
issue_body:
**Note**: The issue affects the Keycloak Operator and the Quarkus distribution

### Description


-   Package Manager: maven
-   Vulnerable module: org.jboss.resteasy:resteasy-multipart-provider
-   Introduced through: org.keycloak:[keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT), org.keycloak:[keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT) and others

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:[keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT) › org.keycloak:[keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT](mailto:keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT) › org.jboss.resteasy:[resteasy-multipart-provider@4.7.7.Final](mailto:resteasy-multipart-provider@4.7.7.Final)

# Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Creation of Temporary File With Insecure Permissions such that the insecure `File.createTempFile()` which is used in the `DataSourceProvider`, `FileProvider` and `Mime4JWorkaround` classes creates temp files with insecure permissions that could be read by a local user.

# Remediation

Upgrade `org.jboss.resteasy:resteasy-multipart-provider` to version 4.7.8.Final or higher.

# References

-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/resteasy/resteasy/commit/277ea6d15b793b53b170ae9d8f9aba83b82d33c8)
-   [GitHub Release](https://github.com/resteasy/resteasy/releases/tag/4.7.8.Final)
-   [RedHat Bugzilla Bug](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2166004)

# Overview

[io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive:resteasy-reactive-common](https://search.maven.org/artifact/io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive/resteasy-reactive-common/3.0.0.Alpha3/jar) is a RESTEasy Reactive - Common Runtime

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Creation of Temporary File With Insecure Permissions via the usage of `File.createTempFile()` class in the `FileBodyHandler` serializer that causes temp files to be created with `-rw-r--r--` permissions.

# Remediation

Update `io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive:resteasy-reactive-common` to 2.16.1.

# References

-   [RedHat Bugzilla Bug](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2163533)

issue_title: UserSessionEntity can contain stale ClientSession references
issue_body:
When a client session expires and the corresponding user session does not, the loaded user session still contains a reference to the already expired client session.

When a client session is loaded, we check whether it is expired; if not, we return null.

The presence of a stale client session reference can cause an issue in the following scenario:
1. Create a user session and a client session where the client session has lower expiration than the user session
2. Move in time after the client session is expired
3. Create a new client session for the same client as in step 1

In step 3 we detect whether the client session already exists for the client and [remove it](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/340025b412d7d14673d55932b87506bc468fabfd/model/map/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/userSession/MapUserSessionProvider.java#L123). This is not executed for HotRod because it detects client session is not present in the cache as it expired.

Then we add the newly created client session. This adds another client session reference for the same `clientId`. In this case, when we obtain a client session for the given client we sometimes (depends on the Set::stream order) hit the expired client session and we return `null` client session because the one we hit is expired.

To fix that, we need to remove the stale reference when we encounter it.

issue_title: `JpaUserProvider` count methods are inconsistent with `searchForUser`'s param filter handling
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

Hello Keycloak team,

I have implemented a custom user federation in Keycloak 20.0.5, which is backed by some external services. Everything was working fine until I added some clients with service accounts enabled. The issue I'm facing is that the **firstResult** parameter is always being calculated incorrectly. Specifically, when I click on the next page in the admin console or hit the admin API with parameters like **first=20&max=20**, the **firstResult** variable value is calculated as **(first - no of service accounts)**. I have tested this many times and confirmed that the issue only arises when clients with service accounts are enabled.

When I remove all clients with service accounts, the **firstResult** parameter is calculated correctly, and everything works as expected. However, this is not a feasible solution for my use case as I need to have service accounts enabled for some clients.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43490933/224482467-da0cc2e3-fb03-417e-a868-180ac0955a97.png)

I believe that this issue is causing problems with my custom user federation implementation and breaking my integration. I would appreciate it if you could look into this issue and provide any guidance or advice on how to resolve it.

Thank you for your help.





### Version

20.0.5, 21.0.1

### Expected behavior

**firstResult** parameter must have the exact value passed from the API call.  

### Actual behavior

**firstResult** parameter is influenced by services accounts

### How to Reproduce?

Just add a custom user federation, a couple of clients with service a/c and click two times on the next page in the admin console  

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Incorrect ldap-group-mapper chosen to sync changes to ActiveDirectory when several mappers with varying group paths used
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

Hi everyone!

I have a KC 15.0.2 setup with the following configuration:
Single LDAP Mapper in WRITABLE mode targeted to ActiveDirectory Domain with multiple OU's where KC-managed groups are contained.
Several (e.g. 4) ldap-group-mapper's with LDAP_ONLY mode configured to different Groups Path's finally resolving groups in two-level hierarchy:
/ (ldap-group-mapper-0 with LDAP Groups DN 0)
/Groups-Path1/ (ldap-group-mapper-1 with LDAP Groups DN 1)
/Groups-Path2/ (ldap-group-mapper-2 with LDAP Groups DN 2)
/Groups-Path3/ (ldap-group-mapper-3 with LDAP Groups DN 3)
On sync'ing the groups from LDAP everything works fine, the groups from subsequent LDAP Groups DN's are successfully imported into configured group paths, but when adding the user in KC GUI to some of the imported groups fails on ActiveDirectory side with error UpdErr: DSID-031A11DA, problem 6005 (ENTRY_EXISTS), data 0 meaning that KC is trying to create or update a group with existing name (cn) in an incorrect OU of the domain and ActiveDirectory does not allow that since group names must be unique throughout the domain. In other words, despite the specifically attributed Group Path the KC is selecting wrong group mapper for updating the group attributes/membership and ActiveDirectory is refusing the update to the group structure as soon as DN is wrong.
This error appears with every group mapper groups except this, related to the "automatically" selected wrong mapper. For the wrong mapper, group attributes/membership management proceeds without any problems.
Another symptom is that with the mentioned setup the current user groups in KC user groups GUI section are shown only for that mapper, while users are shown correctly if listed through KC group members GUI section (therefore the same listings vary in two sections of the GUI).
Please help to resolve this problem or route me to solution if already resolved.

_Originally posted by @dimitry-a-baranov in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/6970#issuecomment-1464884028_

### Version

15.0.2 through 21.0.1

### Expected behavior

1) Adding user someUserName to group with group path /Groups-Path1/somegroupname1 results in successful sync of this change to ActiveDirectory Domain.
2) Adding user someUserName to group with group path /Groups-Path2/somegroupname2 results in successful sync of this change to ActiveDirectory Domain.
3) Adding user someUserName to group with group path /Groups-Path3/somegroupname3 results in successful sync of this change to ActiveDirectory Domain.

### Actual behavior

1) Adding user someUserName to group with group path /Groups-Path1/somegroupname1 results in failed sync of this change to ActiveDirectory Domain:
 UpdErr: DSID-031A11DA, problem 6005 (ENTRY_EXISTS) with stack trace showing that KC is incorrectly trying to create or update somegroupname1 in wrong OU, configured as Groups DN for LDAP Group Mapper from 2) with /Groups-Path2/.
2) Adding user someUserName to group with group path /Groups-Path2/somegroupname2 results in successful sync of this change to ActiveDirectory Domain since Groups DN is correct.
3) Adding user someUserName to group with group path /Groups-Path3/somegroupname3 results in successful sync of this change to ActiveDirectory Domain:
 UpdErr: DSID-031A11DA, problem 6005 (ENTRY_EXISTS) with stack trace showing that KC is incorrectly trying to create or update somegroupname1 in wrong OU, configured as Groups DN for LDAP Group Mapper from 2) with /Groups-Path2/.

### How to Reproduce?

0. Within some test ActiveDirectory Domain create 3 or more OUs under Groups, like TestOU1, TestOU2 and TestOU3, and create within each of them subsequently a local group (testgroup1, testgroup2 and testgroup3), then create a user testuser1.
1. Create a new realm TESTREALM in KC.
2. Create a new "TEST_AD_LDAP" LDAP Provider in User Federation, select Active Directory as Vendor, configure the required values and bind credentials, set Edit Mode to WRITABLE, and configure Search Scope to Subtree.
3. Configure Group Mappers with ldap-group-mapper type to create group hierarchy:
3.1. Configure root-level group mapper ldap-group-mapper-0:
3.1.1. set LDAP Groups DN to OU=Groups,DC=...,etc
3.1.2. set mappedAttributes to "samaccountname,grouptype"
3.1.3. set Mode to LDAP_ONLY
3.1.4. set User Groups Retrieve Strategy to LOAD_GROUPS_BY_MEMBER_ATTRIBUTE
3.2. Create dummy groups for Group Paths:
3.2.1. Use New button to create new group items Groups-Path1, Groups-Path2, Groups-Path3, then set following attributes to each group: samaccountname="Groups-Path1" (2, or 3 subsequently), grouptype="-2147483644", save the attribute values
3.2.2. Within ldap-group-mapper-0 mapper configuration page use Sync Keycloak Groups to LDAP button to create the groups in AD LDAP
3.3. Create and configure lower-level group mappers ldap-group-mapper-1, ldap-group-mapper-2 and ldap-group-mapper-3 with LDAP Groups DN pointing to varying specific different OUs in AD LDAP and subsequent Groups Paths "/Groups-Path1/", "/Groups-Path2/", "/Groups-Path3/", Mode set to LDAP_ONLY, User Groups Retrieve Strategy set to LOAD_GROUPS_BY_MEMBER_ATTRIBUTE, mapped Group Attributes set to "samaccountname,grouptype" and Preserve Group Inheritance set to Off, then save the mapper configuration and use Sync LDAP Groups to Keycloak in each to import the LDAP groups to Keycloak under certain paths.
4. Use Synchronise all users function in TEST_AD_LDAP Provider configuration page to import users from LDAP into Keycloak
5. Select any available user from the list of imported users (e.g. testuser1) and on the Groups tab select any group under /Groups-Path1/ (e.g. /Groups-Path1/testgroup1), then use Join button - Keycloak will throw an Unexpected Error with Log [LDAP: error code 68 - 00000563:  UpdErr: DSID-031A11DA, problem 6005 (ENTRY_EXISTS) , data 0]; and remaining name referencing 'cn=testgroup1,OU=' and OU, configured as LDAP Groups DN within ldap-group-mapper-2.
6. Select the same user from the list of imported users (e.g. testuser1) and on the Groups tab select any group under /Groups-Path2/ (e.g. /Groups-Path2/testgroup2), then use Join button - Keycloak will show "successfully joined".
7. Select any available user from the list of imported users (e.g. testuser1) and on the Groups tab select any group under /Groups-Path3/ (e.g. /Groups-Path3/testgroup3), then use Join button - Keycloak will throw an Unexpected Error with Log [LDAP: error code 68 - 00000563:  UpdErr: DSID-031A11DA, problem 6005 (ENTRY_EXISTS) , data 0]; and remaining name referencing 'cn=testgroup3,OU=' and OU, configured as LDAP Groups DN within ldap-group-mapper-2.
8. Actual order of unsuccessful mappers may vary and I did not find the way KC sorts those mappers, but it would only allow one configured group path (mapper) to be used successfully and the use of any other would lead to the same error.

### Anything else?

In addition to the add/update groups problem I can see that the groups are shown on the corresponding tab of the User UI page within Admin Console for the only group mapper, which is also successfully sync'ed (ldap-group-mapper-2 in the reproduce guide above). Meanwhile on the Group UI page within Admin Console (e.g. testgroup1, testgroup3 in the example above) the same user is shown among Group Members correctly.

issue_title: /users/count endpoint with search field has different behavior than /users query endpoint
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

/users/count endpoint with search field has different behavior than /users query endpoint because of the JpaUserProvider implementation.

In the method 

`searchForUserStream(RealmModel realm, Map<String, String> attributes, Integer firstResult, Integer maxResults)`

the search key/value attribute is trimmed to treat spaces as different search predicates:

```
                case UserModel.SEARCH:
                    for (String stringToSearch : value.trim().split("\\s+")) {
                        predicates.add(builder.or(getSearchOptionPredicateArray(stringToSearch, builder, root)));
                    }
                    break;
```

In the methods

`getUsersCount(RealmModel realm, String search, Set<String> groupIds)`

and

`getUsersCount(RealmModel realm, String search)`

the search attribute is used as a unique predicate:

```
                predicates.add(builder.or(getSearchOptionPredicateArray(search, builder, root)));
```

### Version

>18.0.0

### Expected behavior

Having the user with representation:

```
    {
        "username": "john.doe",
        "firstName": "John",
        "lastName": "Doe Smith",
        "email": "john.doe@keycloak.org",
    }
```

calling the endpoint: 

`/users/count?search=jo do` 

I expect result to be 1.

### Actual behavior

Result is 0

### How to Reproduce?

Call the endpoint:

```
curl --location --request GET 'https://{mydomain}/auth/admin/realms/{myrealm}/users/?search=jo do' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer Token'
```

and 

```
curl --location --request GET 'https://{mydomain}/auth/admin/realms/{myrealm}/users/count?search=jo do' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer Token'
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Can create a user without the registration flow finished properly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

I can create a User entity with the registration flow throwing an Exception. Not all elements are run in the registration flow in such cases, but the user still exists. Neither a REGISTER event is saved for the user.

### Version

21.0.1 (but 19.0.1 is affected as well)

### Expected behavior

If the user entity is created then the registration flow should execute until the end successfully. Probably the whole flow should run in a single transaction.

### Actual behavior

The user entity is created, but an error page is shown to the user. The registration flow is not executed until the end.

### How to Reproduce?

A realm is required where the following is set:
 - registration enabled
 - forgot password enabled

To reproduce:
 - Open the login page
 - Click on the _register_ link
 - Click on the _back to the login_ link
 - Click on the _forgot password_ link
 - Enter a valid, existing e-mail address to the form. Click on the Submit button
 - Press the back button in the browser until you get back to the registration form (~3 times?)
   - a _page is expired_ error will be shown somewhere. Don't care about it, just press the back button until you get back to the login page
 - Fill out the registration form & submit your registration

An error page is shown, but the user is created in the database. From now on the freshly created user can ask for a reset password link, and log in after that with their newly created password.

### Anything else?

I have a fix and will submit a PR in a few minutes.

issue_title: File Map Storage: FileMapKeycloakTransaction.rollback deletes files that shouldn't be deleted
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

`FileMapKeycloakTransaction.rollback` deletes all files registered for deletion. This collection includes both temporary files created during updates and files corresponding to entities that were deleted in the transaction. While deleting the temp files is the right thing to do, deletion of the other files should not be performed on rollback.

So either `rollback` filters out the entity files when deleting, or we use a separate collection to keep track of deleted entities and only remove those on `commit`.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

Rollback should not delete the files corresponding to entities deleted in the transaction. Instead, those deletions should be ignored on rollback.

### Actual behavior

Rollback is behaving in the same way as commit when it comes to deleted entities, leaving the storage in a different state if the transaction is rolled back

### How to Reproduce?

-

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: docker image 21.0.1 lacks a Javascript engine
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Docker image 21.0.1 packs Java 17. Docker images of version 20 and below pack Java version 11 (or lower), which come with a built in Javascript engine. Java 17 doesn't include a Javascript engine, but the Dockerfile hasn't been adjusted accordingly. There are some policies within Keycloak that are implemented using Javascript. Keycloak crashes when it tries to execute these policies.

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

Keycloak is able to execute policies that are implemented using Javascript.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak crashes when trying to execute policies that are implemented using Javascript.
Stacktrace excerpt:

issue_title: Invalid Frontend URL leads to NullPointerException in OIDC Endpoints
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When configuring the "Frontend URL" parameter under `/admin/master/console/#/my-realm/realm-settings` missing the Scheme of the URL leads to a `NullPointerException`, which is hard to pinpoint to an actual cause without looking into the code.

This leads to internal server errors, when requesting resources such as "/realms/my-realm/.well-known/openid-configuration" and also causes errors when authenticating through the realm.

The reason lies in the schema check made by the `RealmsResource`:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/ce1e0a65e7ffdcb3b484aebcadd0456e65cba2fa/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/RealmsResource.java#L275

This line does not place the literal "https" first in the comparison, which then leads to the observed `NullPointerException`.

As the application uses a `java.net.URI` internally the missing schema goes unnoticed and `new URI("example.com").getScheme()` returns `null`.

### Version

20.0.5

### Expected behavior

When I enter "example.com" into the "Frontend URL" field, then the validation of the field should prohibit saving the changes to the realm, because "example.com" is not a valid URL.

When I somehow have already entered an invalid "Frontend URL", then requesting the resources should not fail with a `NullPointerException`.

### Actual behavior

When I enter "example.com" into the "Frontend URL" field, then my mistake is processed without any notice.

When I somehow have already entered an invalid "Frontend URL", then requesting resources, such as "/realms/my-realm/.well-known/openid-configuration" fail with a `500 - Internal server error`.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Select the realm (e.g. "my-realm")
2. Select "Realm Settings"
3. Enter "example.com" into the "Frontend URL" parameter
4. Click the "Save" Button
5. Open "/realms/my-realm/.well-known/openid-configuration"

### Anything else?

This could have been prevented by using static code analysis tools. E.g. https://pmd.github.io/latest/pmd_rules_java_bestpractices.html#literalsfirstincomparisons

Thanks for all the hard work.

issue_body:

### Area

### Describe the bug

If the localStorage is full enough for 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/f6128909e5ac5320e9249cab83a1dbf6cf45b560/adapters/oidc/js/src/keycloak.js#L1619

This can also happen if the localStorage is filled between the time that the check with `kc-test` occurred and actual `cs.add` is called.

### Version

21

### Expected behavior

Setting the item to localStorage should be wrapped in try/catch and QuotaExceeded should be handled - e.g. by switching to CookieStorage or at least communicating the error.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak auth becomes unresponsive and error is logged to devtools.

issue_title: Getting error when trying to access tokens page for a realm in new admin console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I install my custom providers and when I try accessing the Tokens page within Realm settings of any realm I get the error
`p[2].properties[4] is undefined`

This page worked previously on KC 20 with the old (now removed) admin console without any problems.

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

I expected to be able to view and edit token settings for any given realm.


### Actual behavior

I get the error
`p[2].properties[4] is undefined`
and a link that lets me refresh the page and go back to realm settings.

### How to Reproduce?

I have spent a lot of time on this problem. This doesn't happen with a barebones keycloak installation. This happens when I add my own custom providers. I managed to isolate the problematic provider to be my DefaultKeyProvider which extends AbstractRsaKeyProvider which in turn implements KeyProvider.
Once that provider is enabled the problem ensues.
I looked at how data is fetched to display the frontend and I see there's a /auth/admin/serverinfo request which in the response has
componentTypes which has an array org.keycloak.keys.keyProvider of all key providers.
These objects are sorted alphabetically by the id of the key-provider in this case:
"aes-generated", "ecdsa-generated", "hmac-generated" ...
When I add mine with the id "default-key-provider" it gets pushed in between the first two I just listed which come with barebones keycloak. I see most of these keyproviders have an array field called "properties" with going to at least index 4, but I see the one that got pushed 'to the right' - "ecdsa-generated" is index 2 now (previously being index 1 without my default-key-provider). 
This "ecdsa-generated" key provider only has properties going to index 3 so I'm assuming that some frontend code is trying to access item with index 4 of its properties field and getting undefined throwing this error.

Edit:
I forgot to mention originally but I tried a workaround changing the id to be "z-default-key-provider" and it worked. This made it sort last alphabetically and not throw this error.

### Anything else?

I see a similar issue here:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/16032

issue_title: token-exchange does apply clientScopes of the origin client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

The token exchange between an origin-client and a target-client uses the clientScopes of the origin client for token claim mapping, if the dynamic-scope feature is enabled. 
Hence, the custom claims of the tokens are set as defined in the origin-client. All clientScopes and connected protocolMappers/scopes of the target-client are ignored. 

May be connected to #17220

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

token contains claims from target-client
```
{ 
  "aud":` "target-client",
  "azp": "origin-client",
  "target-client": "target-client"
}
```

### Actual behavior

token contains claims from origin-client
```
{ 
 "aud": "target-client",
  "azp": "origin-client",
  "origin-client": "origin-client" <- claim from dedicated-origin-client clientScope
}
```

### How to Reproduce?

Enabled features to reproduce
- DYNAMIC_SCOPES
- TOKEN_EXCHANGE
- ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ

Steps
- Two clients with a token exchange policy from origin client to target client (https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/securing_apps/#_client_to_client_permission)
- Add different ProtocolMapper for the dedicated clientScope of both realms (hard coded claim as example).
- Retrieve token for origin-client.
- Issue a token-exchange with the accessToken of the origin-client with requested_token_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:access_token to the token-api
- only the origin-client claim is present, while the target-client claim is absent


### Anything else?

If one enables the DYNAMIC_SCOPES feature in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/oauth/ClientTokenExchangeTest.java#L84, the testExchangeFromPublicClient will fail with a mismatch between the clientIds (expected: target, actual: realm-management). 
![Bildschirmfoto 2023-03-20 um 16 19 28](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/65015434/226387867-992fd085-6240-4b84-8713-0f479f8f669b.png)

In these code snipets the clientModel of the origin client is used during the token exchange. A suggestion for a fix is to use the client of the authenticationSession instead of the client of the session here @dgozalo :
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9db7c8fb34146a3726a085bd18e25c6c1a731f8a/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/util/AuthorizationContextUtil.java#L68
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9db7c8fb34146a3726a085bd18e25c6c1a731f8a/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/rar/parsers/ClientScopeAuthorizationRequestParserProviderFactory.java#L35
commit_message:
Use the target client when processing scopes for internal exchanges

Closes #19183

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: Docs pom's header-maven-plugin version specification does not work with version alignment
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

In #19158, the documentation module was added to the default build set. The documentation build uses a custom maven plugin, header-maven-plugin, that is built at the start of the docs, and then used in other parts of the doc build.

The problem is that where this plugin is used, it's version is specified with the static `<version>999.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>` rather than `<version>${project.version}</version>` as with the custom licences maven plugin. When version alignment occurs as part of a product build, the project version changes, and therefore will lead to build errors like this:

```
Plugin org.keycloak.documentation:header-maven-plugin:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT or one of its dependencies could not be resolved: Could not find artifact org.keycloak.documentation:header-maven-plugin:jar:999.0.0-SNAPSHOT
```

This is because the plugin has been built under a different version number.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

The maven build should complete without errors when trying to build a product version

### Actual behavior

The build fails due to documentation being now included in the default modules, and having the described problem in it's pom.xml

### How to Reproduce?

Either run the PME CLI, or manually change all the project versions without touching this version

### Anything else?

_No response_

Specify header-maven-plugin version via project.version property

issue_title: Slow Query Caused By Composite Indexes Order On Broker Link Table
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

"broker_link" table has Multiple-column index as follow:
 ` <addPrimaryKey columnNames="IDENTITY_PROVIDER, USER_ID" constraintName="CONSTR_BROKER_LINK_PK" tableName="BROKER_LINK" />`

And the read query in "broker_entity"  is as follows: 
`@NamedQuery(name= "findBrokerLinkByUserAndProvider", query="select link from BrokerLinkEntity link where link.userId = :userId and link.identityProvider = :identityProvider and link.realmId = :realmId")`

The read query conditions order causes slow query in database transaction.
 

### Version

15.0.1

### Expected behavior

Regarding to [mysql documentation](https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/multiple-column-indexes.html) Multiple-column index should be used in right order to enhance the speed of query!  

So read query condition in "broker_entity" should be as follows:
`@NamedQuery(name= "findBrokerLinkByUserAndProvider", query="select link from BrokerLinkEntity link where link.identityProvider = :identityProvider and link.userId = :userId and link.realmId = :realmId")`


### Actual behavior

Using wrong order of index is causing slow query and high latency in mySQL database! 

### How to Reproduce?

By measuring the speed of identity provider login can check the difference!

### Anything else?

Here is an example of how query search condition order effects the query speed in composite pk tables:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/26255039/mysql-column-order-in-composite-key
commit_message:
Fix broker link query param order to match the columns order in the primary key index

Closes #19193

issue_title: Dist build phase can sometimes fail due to a race condition
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

The [`configureKeycloakSessionFactory`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/8a2d645dd4aaee061aae2598445d7eea1beffef8/quarkus/deployment/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/deployment/KeycloakProcessor.java#L362) build phase requires the [Profile](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/8a2d645dd4aaee061aae2598445d7eea1beffef8/quarkus/deployment/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/deployment/KeycloakProcessor.java#L225) to be initialized. Sometimes, Profile was not ready at a time when `configureKeycloakSessionFactory` was executed.

### Version

21.0.0-21.0.1

### Expected behavior

Build passes

### Actual behavior

```
[ERROR] Failed to execute goal io.quarkus:quarkus-maven-plugin:2.13.7.Final:build (default) on project keycloak-quarkus-server-app: Failed to build quarkus application: io.quarkus.builder.BuildException: Build failure: Build failed due to errors
[ERROR] 	[error]: Build step org.keycloak.quarkus.deployment.KeycloakProcessor#configureKeycloakSessionFactory threw an exception: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot read field "features" because the return value of "org.keycloak.common.Profile.getInstance()" is null
[ERROR] 	at org.keycloak.common.Profile.isFeatureEnabled(Profile.java:189)
[ERROR] 	at org.keycloak.cluster.infinispan.InfinispanClusterProviderFactory.isSupported(InfinispanClusterProviderFactory.java:197)
[ERROR] 	at org.keycloak.provider.EnvironmentDependentProviderFactory.isSupported(EnvironmentDependentProviderFactory.java:44)
[ERROR] 	at org.keycloak.quarkus.deployment.KeycloakProcessor.isEnabled(KeycloakProcessor.java:791)
[ERROR] 	at org.keycloak.quarkus.deployment.KeycloakProcessor.loadFactories(KeycloakProcessor.java:688)
[ERROR] 	at org.keycloak.quarkus.deployment.KeycloakProcessor.configureKeycloakSessionFactory(KeycloakProcessor.java:364)
[ERROR] 	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
[ERROR] 	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:77)
[ERROR] 	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)
[ERROR] 	at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:568)
[ERROR] 	at io.quarkus.deployment.ExtensionLoader$3.execute(ExtensionLoader.java:909)
[ERROR] 	at io.quarkus.builder.BuildContext.run(BuildContext.java:281)
[ERROR] 	at org.jboss.threads.ContextHandler$1.runWith(ContextHandler.java:18)
[ERROR] 	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2449)
[ERROR] 	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1478)
[ERROR] 	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:833)
[ERROR] 	at org.jboss.threads.JBossThread.run(JBossThread.java:501)
[ERROR] -> [Help 1]
```

See also e.g. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/4459029380/jobs/7831298797

### How to Reproduce?

To trigger the race condition, put a thread sleep [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/8a2d645dd4aaee061aae2598445d7eea1beffef8/quarkus/deployment/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/deployment/KeycloakProcessor.java#L633).

### Anything else?

Probable cause: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/f91ac2970d3aa55c0308fefc5b45310e0625b15f#diff-6ebe7f8db3e4dea9dbefd50eed883b8aac997e6c01127e283631e92e9d022835

Weird thing is that even though `configureKeycloakSessionFactory` has `@Consume(ProfileBuildItem.class)`, it does not really care it is never fulfilled.

Fix dist build race condition

issue_title: Disable Groovy Closures when bootstrapping Picocli
issue_body:
### Description

I noticed that during the startup Picocli tries to load Groovy-related classes as per https://github.com/remkop/picocli/blob/main/src/main/java/picocli/CommandLine.java#L5615.

This introduces additional/unnecessary frames in flame graphs and has an additional cost in start-up (even if a few ms).

As we are still behind the latest changes (and the above code) from Picocli and we probably want to add the following system property to our scripts:

```
-Dpicocli.disable.closures=true
```

We should be able to solve this issue once we upgrade to Quarkus 3.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Improve start-up time.

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: Custom implemention of OIDC Login Protocol doesn't get executed
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

We run Keycloak as an identity broker for a large organisation that has currently 40+ clients from external organisation that use a raft of different version of the OIDC and SAML specs.  Up until KC 21.0.x we have been able to get our version to work by declaring the new Login Protocol extending the OOT version, creating a new  org.keycloak.protocol.LoginProtocolMapper services file with our implementation defined within and deploying to the providers folder.  In KC 21 our version now doesn't get executed even though we get a warning at startup about "This SPI is internal and may change without notice".

To be able to service all these Clients we have had to make some minor changes to the OOT OIDCLoginProtocol class found in the keycloak-service module and we package it in our application. I have followed the Service Provider Interfaces (SPI) to make sure we were still doing it correctly.

Can this still be done in the new KC 21.0.x version? if so how can it be done as we don't have the ability to get our clients to change and we need some of the features provided in newer version.

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

When extending the existing OIDCLoginProtocol class with our own and defining the SPI services file and deploying the code into the providers folder would expect to see our code executed rather than the OOTB version.

### Actual behavior

Our version of the OIDCLoginProtocol is loaded by the classloader and a warning is put in the logs (as epected) but the class is never executed when an OIDC login occurs.  We are able to test locally with breakpoints and never see our code executed, only the OOTB version is executed.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a new Class TestOIDCLoginProtocol that extends OIDCLoginProtocol, do the same for the OIDCLoginProtocolFactory ensuring that the new classes are named correctly and reference the correct classes.
2. Create  new file called org.keycloak.protocol.LoginProtoclFactory and place in it the fully qualified name to the TestOIDCLoginProtocolFactory (e.g.  org.test.oidc.TestOIDCLoginProtocolFactory)
3. Package this as a jar and copy to the providers folder within the keycloak installation.
4. Build - kc.bat build
5. Start - kc.bat start-dev --debug  (and whatever else is required)
6. Look in the logs to see:
     [org.keycloak.services] (build-30) KC-SERVICES0047: openid-connect (org.test.oidc.TestOIDCLoginProtocol) is implementing the internal SPI login-protocol. This SPI is internal and may change without notice
7. Create a OIDC Based client and configure approriately
8. attempt to authenticate with the client.  New instance isn't executed.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Custom implemention of OIDC Login Protocol doesn't get executed
closes #19335

issue_title: Sending 'application/jwt' Accept header to GET userinfo endpoint returns a 406 error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

We are using Express JS with passport and the [openid-client](https://www.npmjs.com/package/openid-client) strategy to authenticate to our keycloak environment.

It was working correctly on version 15.0.2, but when upgrading to the latest version (21.0.1) it started failing. After debugging all our code, we realized the issue was because the userinfo endpoint from keycloak is returning a 406 HTTP error code with the following message:
`RESTEASY003635: No match for accept header`

It seems passport/openid-client is sending an Accept header with 'application/jwt', and for some reason the userinfo endpoint is returning an error because of that. 
By doing some hardcoding in passport we were able to send that same request but with the Accept header set to 'application/json' and another try with no Accept header at all, and both resulted in keycloak correctly returning a JWT token with the content-type header set to 'application/jwt', which makes no sense if the Accept with the same mimetype is being rejected.

### Version

21.0.1

### Expected behavior

Get userinfo endpoint should be returning a JWT when we send an Accept header set with 'application/jwt'

### Actual behavior

Get userinfo endpoint is returning a 406 with the message "RESTEASY003635: No match for accept header" when sending an accept header with 'application/jwt'.

This does not happen when sending 'application/json' or no accept header at all. In these cases, the endpoint does return with an 'application/jwt' content-type anyways.

### How to Reproduce?

With a client that returns a JWT token on userinfo (we are using RS256 encryption for the sign), call the GET userinfo endpoint for that client with an Accept header set to 'application/jwt'.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Allow application/jwt media type for userinfo endpoint
Closes: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/19346

issue_title: Incorrect documentation around password policies
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

Latest docs for password policies https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_password-policies mentions this:
```
After saving the policy, Keycloak enforces the policy for new users and sets an Update Password action for existing users to 
ensure they change their password the next time they log in. 
```

This is not correct as Keycloak does NOT enforce existing users to update their password. For example assume that administrator adds new password policy `Minimum length 8` and there is already user `john` with password `foo`.

During the subsequent logins after password policy change, the user is able to login with this password and he is *not* forced to update it during login. However when he wants to update his password (for example in account console), he *is*enforced to use password according to password policy and hence his password must be 8 chars or longer. This also applies for all newly registered users (after password policy was created).

So what we can do is:
- Update docs and clarify this (for example add warning that admins should set their password policy from the beginning of the realm and/or add "Update password" to existing users and/or use "Expire password" to make sure that users update their passwords in next N days, which will in fact adjust to new password policies)
- Implement something to make sure that existing users are forced to update passwords as well. There is related issue #14150

### Version

21.0.1
